info.title: Timbal Platform API
info.description: Public API documentation for the Timbal platform

get.description: List jobs for an ACE, most recent first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return (clamped to 200). Defaults to 50.

get.description: Fetch a single job. Poll this until `status` reaches a terminal value (`succeeded`, `failed`, `cancelled`, `error`).

post.description: Cancel an active job. The job transitions to `status = 'cancelled'` once the cancellation takes effect. Returns `204` on accept; `409` if the job is not currently active.

Re-enqueue a failed/cancelled/errored job with the same kind and input, returning a new `job_uid`. Returns `409` if the source job is still active or already succeeded, or if another job is active on this ACE.

409.description: Source job not retriable, or ACE has a live job

get.description: List the policies declared on an ACE.

post.description: OpenAI-compatible **Chat Completions** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` request body; set `stream: true` for an SSE response. The upstream provider is resolved from the `model` field: either explicit (`anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, `google/gemini-2.5-pro`, …) or best-effort from the model name prefix (`gpt-*`, `claude-*`, `gemini-*`, `grok-*`); otherwise falls back to OpenAI. Supported providers: `openai`, `google`, `togetherai`, `xai`, `groq`, `fireworks`, `xiaomi`, `byteplus`. Request body remains OpenAI-shaped regardless of provider. Authenticate with a Timbal API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

post.description: Anthropic-compatible **Messages** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native Anthropic `/v1/messages` request body; set `stream: true` for an SSE response. The `model` field accepts either a bare model name (`claude-opus-4-7`) or an explicit `anthropic/<model>` prefix; any other provider prefix is rejected with `400`. Authenticate with a Timbal API key passed in the `x-api-key` header (same header the Anthropic SDK sends). Optional `anthropic-version` and `anthropic-beta` headers are forwarded upstream. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

post.description: OpenAI-compatible **Responses API** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native OpenAI `/v1/responses` request body; streaming responses are delivered as SSE. The upstream provider is resolved from the `model` field: either explicit (`google/gemini-2.5-pro`, `xai/grok-3`, …) or best-effort from the model name prefix (`gpt-*`, `gemini-*`, `grok-*`); otherwise falls back to OpenAI. Supported providers: `openai`, `google`, `togetherai`, `xai`, `groq`, `fireworks`. Request body remains OpenAI-shaped regardless of provider. Authenticate with a Timbal API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

post.description: Upload a short-lived file (auto-deleted ~24h after upload).

file.description: Binary contents of the file. Max 100 MB.

400.description: Validation failed or non-nullable field set to null

post.description: Create a new knowledge base

get.description: Get knowledge base details
List backups for a knowledge base.

post.description: Create an on-demand backup of a knowledge base. While running, all read/write traffic on the KB returns 503 (Maintenance). A mutually-exclusive lock prevents concurrent backup/restore on the same KB (409 Conflict). `dry_run = true` skips the lock and the upload.

201.description: Backup accepted; KB is already paused for maintenance (non-dry-run). Poll via GET /backups/{id}.

409.description: KB is already under maintenance (no backup row created)

get.description: Get a single backup.

post.description: Create a virtual directory in the knowledge base

409.description: A file with the same name already exists at this path

get.description: List files and directories in a knowledge base

post.description: Add a file to a knowledge base via direct upload or remote URL fetch. Provide exactly one of `file` (raw bytes) or `url` (http/https; the server fetches it with an SSRF-hardened client).

schema.description:
Request body for adding a file to a knowledge base.

Provide **either** `file` (raw bytes via multipart) **or** `url` (http/https URL
the server fetches directly). Exactly one is required.

directory.description: Optional subdirectory path to organize the file within the knowledge base.

file.description: File bytes (multipart `file` field). Mutually exclusive with `url`.

metadata.description: Optional JSON metadata to attach to the file.

url.description:
Public http/https URL the server fetches and ingests directly.
SSRF-hardened (internal hosts blocked). Mutually exclusive with `file`.

RateLimit-Limit.description: Max requests per window for this user.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

409.description: A file with the same name already exists in this directory

RateLimit-Limit.description: Max requests per window for this user.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

Retry-After.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying.

get.description: Get a file with its parsings and embeddings metadata

200.description: File details with parsings and embeddings

delete.description: Delete a file from a knowledge base

get.description: List chunks for a knowledge base file, in document order. Layout metadata (`blocks`) is omitted from each item to keep responses small; fetch a single chunk by `uid` to retrieve it.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description:
Cursor: pass `next_page_token` from the previous response to fetch
the next page. Treat the value as opaque.

post.description: Insert a new chunk. Place via `after_uid` / `before_uid` (mutually exclusive) or omit both to append.

201.description: Chunk inserted. Search results reflect the change after re-indexing completes (`index_status` field).

409.description: No insertion slot available between the specified neighbors. Retry with a different placement.

get.description: Get a single chunk by uid.

delete.description: Delete a chunk. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET. The chunk disappears from listings immediately and stops affecting search after the next re-index pass.

412.description: `If-Match` revision does not match the current chunk

patch.description: Edit a chunk's content / embed / page range. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET.

200.description: Chunk updated. Search results reflect the change after re-indexing completes (`index_status` field).

412.description: `If-Match` revision does not match the current chunk

post.description: Execute a SQL query against the knowledge base
Drop the knowledge base's in-memory connections so the next request opens a fresh database handle. Caller must be an org admin.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

403.description: Caller is not an admin of this knowledge base

get.description: List restore attempts for a knowledge base.

post.description: Restore a knowledge base from a previously completed backup. While running, all read/write traffic on the KB returns 503 (Maintenance). A mutually-exclusive lock prevents concurrent backup/restore on the same KB (409 Conflict).

201.description: Restore accepted; KB is already paused for maintenance. Poll via GET /restores/{id}.

409.description: KB is already under maintenance (no restore row created)

get.description: Get a single restore.
Get the schema for a knowledge base

200.description: Schema (structured by default, or SQL DDL statements when ?format=sql)

oneOf.description: Structured schema with tables, columns, indexes, and constraints (default)
SQL DDL statements (CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, etc.)

schema.description: Response for the schema endpoint — structured (default) or SQL DDL.

post.description: Upload a data file into the knowledge base via direct upload or remote URL fetch

requestBody.description: Multipart form with either a `file` field (raw bytes) OR a `url` field (http/https; the server fetches it). Optional `table_name` overrides the auto-derived table name; optional `mode` is `create` (default), `replace`, or `append`.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Exclusive range end (epoch milliseconds). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Restrict to a specific user’s runs.

get.description: Time-binned run counts, user counts, and duration percentiles for a project.
Per-user run and spend aggregates for a project.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque offset for the next page (from the previous response’s `next_page_token`).

post.description: Send a prompt to the Timbal Composer agent. The `prompt` field accepts a plain string, an array of content blocks, or a full Message object. File blocks (`base64` or `url` source) are staged server-side under the session inbox and replaced with a path-reference text block before reaching the model — bytes never enter the conversation context. The agent can then either `Read` the staged file for inspection, `import_kb_table` it to create queryable DuckDB tables (csv/xlsx/parquet/json), or `add_kb_file` to parse + embed it for semantic/keyword search (pdf/docx/md/txt/audio). Legacy `.xls` is not accepted for tabular ingest; save as `.xlsx` first.

prompt: Load this into the knowledge base as table 'sales' (append mode)

value.prompt: Add error handling to the upload endpoint
How would you refactor the upload endpoint for better error handling?

prompt: Describe what you see in this screenshot and fix the layout

get.description: List deployments for a project

parameters.name: project_env_id
parameters.description:
DEPRECATED: use `rev` (git branch name) instead. Will be removed once all
frontends migrate. When both are passed, `rev` wins.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description:
Git branch name of the env to filter by (e.g. `main`). Resolved
server-side against `ProjectEnvs.branch` for the current project.

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.description:
Optional trigger filter. Accepts:
  * `latest` — return every row sharing the most recent `trigger_id` for
    the resolved env; if the most recent row has no `trigger_id` (legacy
    data), falls back to `DISTINCT ON (image_type, manifest_id)` i.e.
    the latest row per component.
  * a UUID — return every row with `trigger_id = <uuid>`.
Requires an env filter (`rev` or `project_env_id`). No-op otherwise.

List workforce components for the project. When `rev` is provided the response is scoped to that git branch and includes deployment URLs; when omitted all registered components are returned.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`). When omitted, all registered (non-deleted) workforce components are returned without deployment status.

post.description: Create a workforce component (agent or workflow) on the git **branch** given by `rev`.

delete.description: Delete a workforce component on the git **branch** given by `rev`.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

put.description: Link an existing ACE to a workforce component. Replaces any previously linked ACE.

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

post.description: Create a new ACE attached to a workforce component and enqueue the initial generation job, seeded from the component's current source on the given branch. Returns `202` immediately with `{ace_uid, job_uid}`; poll `GET /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}` for terminal state. The ACE's name defaults to `{project_name}/{workforce_name}`.

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

delete.description: Detach the ACE currently attached to a workforce component. The ACE itself is preserved and can be reattached to any workforce component. Active jobs on the ACE are NOT cancelled — use `POST /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}/cancel` if that's what you want.

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

get.description: Proxy HTTP to a **running** workforce deployment (agent or workflow). Non-serverless: method and headers pass through; JSON on `/run` or `/stream` may get `context.platform_config` merged for SDK auth-back. Serverless: no upstream URL — the platform runs the workforce for this request. Pass `workforce` as id, manifest UUID, or name; `rev` is the git branch. Response header `X-Timbal-Workforce-Deployment-Type` is `serverless`, `ecs`, or `on-premise`. WebSocket upgrades are not supported.

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path on the deployment server after the component segment (for example `run` or `healthcheck`). Leave empty to call the deployment root.

404.description: No running deployment found for this component on this branch

502.description: The deployment was unreachable, dropped the connection, or returned an invalid response

504.description: The deployment did not respond within the proxy timeout

Response header `X-Timbal-Workforce-Deployment-Type` is `serverless`, `ecs`, or `on-premise`. WebSocket upgrades are not supported.

409.description: A role with that name already exists in the organization

delete.description: Delete a custom role. Fails with 409 if any member currently holds the role unless `force: true`, in which case those members are demoted to the system `user` role before the delete proceeds. System roles cannot be deleted.

requestBody.description: Optional — omit entirely or send `{}` for default (non-force) behavior.

409.description: Role is still assigned to one or more members

AceJobView.description: Public view of a background job against an ACE.

error_message.description: Present when the job terminated unsuccessfully (`failed`, `error`, `cancelled`).

AcePolicy.description:
A single policy entry.

- `id` is the human-readable stable key (e.g. `"ev_subsidy"`).
- `requires` / `provides` / `tool_action` are kept as raw JSON because
  their shape is still evolving.

AceVar.description:
A single variable declaration.

- `id` is the human-readable stable key (e.g. `"flow"`, `"presupuesto_max"`).
- `choices`, when present, restricts the variable to an enum of string values.
- `match_mode` tweaks how the matcher compares extracted vs expected values
  (e.g. `"semantic"`).

Action.description:
A domain action a role can grant. Action keys follow
`<scope>.<resource>.<verb>` (e.g. `org.project.create`).

AppType.description:
Read/output enum for a workforce component's type.

Tolerant on deserialization (`#[serde(other)]`) because this type is reconstructed
from DB rows via two paths that both must not fail on unknown values:
  1. [`AppType::try_from`] — used by `FromRow` impls reading `OrgsApps.type` directly.
  2. `serde_json::from_value` — used by handlers that build workforce JSON via
     `json_build_object('type', oa.type, ...)` (runs list, deployments, etc.) and
     then deserialize into `WorkforcePreview` / `RunPreview`.

New workforce types written by `on_push` (or older ones removed from this enum) must
not break list/get endpoints, hence `Unknown` as a catch-all.

**Do not use this for API request bodies** — see [`AppTypeInput`] for the strict
input variant that rejects unknowns at the deserialization boundary.

AppTypeInput.description:
Strict input variant of [`AppType`] for API request bodies (e.g. `ProjectOrigin::Scratch`).
Rejects unknown strings and the literal `"unknown"` at deserialization (→ 400).

cap_credits.description: Max overage **credits** the user allows (null = unlimited).

ChannelAction.description: How the rule was wired to `NotificationChannels` for this create call.

ComposeEffort.description: Reasoning effort level for the session.

ComposeModel.description: Model family to use for the session. Always resolves to the latest released version.

oneOf.description:
Reasoning effort level. Higher levels let the model think longer before responding,
at proportionally higher cost and latency.

Session behavior mode. `default` bypasses all tool-permission prompts and lets the agent
edit files, run shell commands, and call MCP tools freely. `plan` restricts the agent to
read-only tools — it analyses the codebase and produces a plan, without mutating anything.

Model family to use. Always resolves to the latest version of that family —
specific version pinning is not exposed.

prompt.description: The prompt: a plain string, an array of content blocks, or a full Message object.

rev.description: Branch to run against (worktree is checked out at this rev)

session_id.description: Existing session ID to continue a conversation. Omit to start a new session.

dry_run.description: When omitted or when the request has no JSON body, treated as `false`.

config.description: Omitted or null when linking by `id` only.

id.description: Existing `NotificationChannels.id` for this org. When set, `type` / `config` / `name` are ignored.

name.description: Name for the new knowledge base

directory.description: Folder path, same format as the `directory` field on file upload (e.g. `docs/reports`).

created.description: `true` if this call created the folder, `false` if it already existed.

placeholder_file_id.description: Listing id for this folder; remove it with `DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/k2/{kb_id}/files/{file_id}`.

CreateNotificationChannelReq.description: Body for **POST** `/orgs/{org_id}/notifications/channels` (standalone channel; same validation as inline rule channel, no `id`).

CreateNotificationRuleReq.description: Body for POST org-wide or project-scoped notification rules (same shape).

channel.description: Channel: inline `type` + `config`, or reuse with `{ "id": <channel id> }` (same org).

actions.description:
Domain actions this role grants. Unknown action keys fail
deserialization (400). Empty list is allowed.

name.description: Role name. Must be unique within the organization.
Optional display name for the new component. If omitted, a unique name is generated.

CreateWorkforceResponse.description: Response returned when a new workforce component is created.

path.description: Path to the new component inside the repository worktree (for example `workforce/my-agent`).

uid.description: Identifier assigned in the project manifest for this component.

CreditGrant.description: A single credit grant on an organization.

amount_credits.description:
Credits awarded by this grant. Always positive; corrections ship as a
separate grant rather than a negative amount.

consumed_credits.description:
Credits already drawn from this grant by attributed costs. Bounded by
`amount_credits`; remaining headroom is `amount_credits - consumed_credits`.

exhausted_at.description:
Set once the grant has been fully consumed by usage; `null` while
the grant still has unspent credits or hasn't been reconciled yet.

expires_at.description: When the grant stops being spendable. `null` = never expires.

granted_by_user_id.description: User who triggered the grant. `null` for system-issued grants.

starts_at.description: When the grant became (or will become) spendable.

uid.description: Stable public identifier for this grant.

force.description: When `true`, delete even if rules reference this channel (cascading unlink).

When `true`, demote any members currently holding this role to the
system `user` role and proceed with the delete.

workforce.description: Component to delete: numeric id, manifest UUID (`uid`), or configured name.

project_env.description: Project environment this domain is linked to, if any

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp in epoch milliseconds

validation_records.description: DNS records required for SSL certificate validation

auto_processing_enabled.description: Toggle the automatic parsing, chunking, and embedding pipeline for new file uploads. When false, uploads are stored as-is. Existing files are not affected.

banner_url.description: URL of the organization's banner image

photo_url.description: URL of the organization's profile photo

role.description:
System role name. Standard values are `admin`, `developer`,
`billing`, and `user`. Mutually exclusive with `role_id`.

role_id.description:
Role identifier — either a system role or a custom role defined
in this organization. Mutually exclusive with `role`.

actions.description: Replace the full action grant set. Omitted fields are left unchanged.

name.description: Role name. Must remain unique within the organization.

EnqueueJobResBody.description:
Response body returned by any endpoint that enqueues a new job. The
HTTP status is `202 Accepted` — poll `GET /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}`
for terminal state.

last_deployment_status.description: Status of the latest deployment in this environment

media_type.description: MIME type. Inferred from `name` extension when omitted.

name.description:
Filename (e.g. "screenshot.png"). Used for the staged file name on disk
and for media-type inference. Optional — when omitted, `media_type` must
be provided explicitly and a generic name is generated.

source.description: How to obtain the file bytes.

after_uid.description:
Place the new chunk immediately after this `chunk_uid`. Mutually
exclusive with `before_uid`. If neither is set, the chunk is
appended at the end of the file.

before_uid.description:
Place the new chunk immediately before this `chunk_uid`. Mutually
exclusive with `after_uid`.

embed.description:
Text used for vector / full-text indexing. Defaults to `content`
when omitted; provide an explicit value to keep an
indexing-optimized variant distinct from the user-visible text.

blocks.description:
Source-document layout metadata (e.g. bounding boxes, page coords)
when available. `null` for chunks created or edited by the user.

index_status.description:
Indexing state. One of `pending`, `indexing`, `indexed`, `failed`.
While not `indexed`, the chunk's contribution to search results may
be stale.

ordinal.description:
Ordering key. Chunks in a file are returned in ascending `ordinal`
order. Treat the value as opaque — placement on insert is controlled
by `after_uid` / `before_uid`, not by setting `ordinal` directly.

revision.description:
Optimistic concurrency token. Send back as `If-Match: <revision>`
on the next mutation (`PATCH` / `DELETE`).

K2ChunkSummary.description:
List-endpoint payload. Same fields as [`K2Chunk`] except `blocks`,
which is omitted to keep list responses small. Fetch a single chunk
by `uid` to retrieve `blocks`.

columns.description: Columns involved (for PK, UNIQUE, NOT_NULL). Empty for CHECK.

auto_processing_enabled.description: When false, file uploads to this knowledge base are stored as-is and skipped by the automatic parsing, chunking, and embedding pipeline.

parse_state.description:
Pipeline state for parsing + embedding.

One of `pending` (pipeline in flight), `success` (parse + embed both completed),
`failed` (latest attempt failed), or `skipped` (format unsupported, never parsed).
Unsupported files are stored as-is; the indicator lets clients flag them in listings
rather than rejecting the upload.

statements.description: DDL statements (CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, etc.)

LinkAceReqBody.description: Request body for linking an existing ACE to a workforce.

ace_uid.description: `uid` of an existing ACE in the same organization.

kb_id.description: ID of the K2 knowledge base to link to this project

ListOrgNotificationRulesResBody.title: ListOrgNotificationRulesResponse
ListOrgNotificationRulesResBody.description: Wrapper for **GET** `/orgs/{org_id}/notifications/rules` — org-scoped rules only (`project_id` is null in storage).

roles.description: Roles available to this organization, system roles first.

next_page_token.description: Token to fetch the next page, or null if this is the last page

org_users.description: List of users in the organization

ListPreviewsResponse.description: Previews currently visible for the organization.

ListWorkforceResBody.description: Workforce components registered for the project. The `url` field is set only when the component has a **running** deployment for the git **branch** you queried (`rev`).

plan_cancel_at.description:
Unix timestamp (seconds) of the scheduled plan cancellation; `null` when
no cancellation is pending.

plan_cancel_at_period_end.description:
`true` between the moment a user schedules a plan cancellation in the
Billing Portal and the final cancellation taking effect.

title.description: Optional job title (e.g. "Senior AI Solutions Architect").

type.description: Role this contact plays for the org.

primary.description: Marks the preferred channel of this kind when multiple are listed.

value.description:
Channel-specific identifier: email address, E.164 phone number, slack
handle, calendly URL, etc. Format is enforced by the channel kind.

OrgContactType.description:
Role a contact plays for the org. Free-form on the wire/DB so new roles
can be introduced without a migration; unknown values fall back to `Other`.

banner_url.description: URL of the organization's banner image

n_projects.description: Number of projects in the organization

photo_url.description: URL of the organization's profile photo

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the organization

id.description: Unique user identifier. Null for pending invites that haven't been accepted yet

photo_url.description: URL of the user's profile photo

embed.description:
Text used for vector / full-text indexing. When `content` is
provided and `embed` is omitted, `embed` is auto-set to the new
`content`. Send an explicit value to keep an indexing-optimized
variant distinct from the user-visible text.

page_start.description:
Tri-state: omit to leave unchanged, send `null` to clear, send an
integer to set.

content.description: Raw markdown body of the plan file.

PlanDetail.description: A plan markdown file plus its full body.

component.description: Which part of the preview this row describes: `api` (backend dev server) or `ui` (frontend dev server).

healthy.description: Whether the dev server answered a health check on its port. Absent when no check was performed.

project_id.description: Included on organization-wide preview listings so you can tell which project a row belongs to.

rev.description: Git branch name this preview is tied to.

slug.description: Opaque token used with your organization’s preview hostname.

started_at.description: UTC timestamp when acceptance of the preview start was recorded.

url.description: Public HTTPS URL for the running preview, when preview hosting is configured for your organization.

default_role.description: Project's default role. All organization members will be assigned this role by default for this project

environments.description: Project environments with their latest deployment status

has_ui.description: Whether the project has a UI interface

is_public_template.description: Whether the project is a public template

knowledge_bases.description: Knowledge bases connected to this project (new KB system only)

publishable_api_key.description: Publishable API key for this project

repository_url.description: URL of the project's source code repository. When null, the project does not have source code available

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the project

screenshot_url.description: URL of the project's screenshot image

template_uses.description: Number of times this template has been used

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp in epoch milliseconds

use_platform_iam.description: Whether the project uses the platform's IAM system for access control

kb_id.description:
Knowledge base to attach to the project. Provide an existing KB's
id to link it, or omit to auto-create an empty one.

ProjectPlan.description:
A plan markdown file produced by a compose `plan` mode session. Plans live
on EFS at `{EFS}/orgs/{org_id}/projects/{project_id}/.claude/plans/*.md`
and are authored by the Claude CLI — the API only reads them.

preview.description: Short body preview (first non-empty paragraph after the title, trimmed).

title.description: Title parsed from the first `# ` heading (with an optional `Plan: ` prefix stripped).

default_role.description: Project's default role. All organization members will be assigned this role by default for this project

is_public_template.description: Whether the project is a public template

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the project

screenshot_url.description: URL of the project's screenshot image

template_uses.description: Number of times this template has been used

first_prompt.description: Truncated text of the first user prompt — used as a session title.

rev.description: Git branch the session is scoped to

tool_call_count.description: Total tool_use blocks across all calls

explain.description: When true, runs EXPLAIN ANALYZE and includes the query plan in the response.

params.description:
Optional parameters for the query. Parameters passed to the vector
search functions (`lance_vector_search`, `lance_hybrid_search`) are
automatically converted to embedding vectors before the query runs.

sql.description:
SQL query to execute against the knowledge base.
Use $1, $2, etc. for parameter placeholders.

email.description: Email address of the user to remove from the organization

access_request_data.description:
Free-form access-request metadata. Merged (shallow) into the existing
stored object server-side.

name.description: Display name. 1–64 characters. Cannot be set to null.

phone.description: Phone number. Pass null to clear.

photo_url.description: Profile photo URL. Pass null to clear.

RoleDetail.description: A role with its full set of granted actions.

actions.description: Domain actions this role grants. May be empty.

id.description:
Stable role identifier. Pass as `role_id` to
`PATCH /orgs/{org_id}/users` to assign this role to a member.

name.description: Role name. Unique within the organization (and globally for system roles).

RoleInUseConflict.description:
Returned when `DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/roles/{role_id}` is called while the
role is still assigned and `force` is not set.

pending_invite_count.description: Pending invites (`user_id` not yet set) still pointing at this role.

user_ids.description: Accepted members currently holding this role.

RolePreview.description:
Compact role record returned by listing endpoints. Fetch a single
role to retrieve the full action grant set.

id.description:
Stable role identifier. Pass as `role_id` to
`PATCH /orgs/{org_id}/users` to assign this role to a member.

name.description: Role name. Unique within the organization (and globally for system roles).

RoleSource.description:
Whether a role is a built-in preset or defined by the organization.
`system` roles are read-only; `custom` roles can be edited or deleted
by an organization admin.

messages.description: Message history from the Claude session file (same format as compose stream events)

name.description: When null, the branch will be cleared

force.description:
If true and the org preview concurrency cap is reached, evict the
oldest running (or in-flight) preview in the org to free a slot
instead of returning 429. Defaults to false.

rev.description: Git branch name to run the preview for (for example `main`).
Git branch name you used when starting the preview (for example `main`).

TempFile.description:
Stateless temporary file metadata. Stored under a dedicated `tmp/` prefix
in object storage and removed by storage lifecycle policy ~24h after upload.
No database row is created; clients must persist the URL/name themselves
if they need to track it.

content_length.description: Size of the uploaded payload in bytes.

content_type.description: MIME type as reported by the client.

expires_at.description:
Advisory expiration (~24h after upload). Object deletion is performed by
storage lifecycle and may lag this timestamp by up to a day. Treat HTTP
404 from `url` as "expired".

name.description: Original filename (sanitized — slashes / control characters replaced).

url.description: Public URL where the file can be downloaded until it expires.

deleted_at.description: Set when the workforce component has been deleted. Only returned from endpoints that include soft-deleted rows (e.g. runs list).

id.description: Identifier of this workforce component within the project.

type.description: Component category (for example agent or workflow).

uid.description: Stable identifier from the project manifest (`timbal.yaml`), when defined.

url.description: When present, the authenticated Timbal API URL used to call this component’s **running** deployment (for example from the list-workforce response). Omitted where the response does not expose a callable URL, such as on project detail.

bearer_auth.description: Timbal API key. Obtain your API key from the Timbal platform settings. See [Authentication](/api-reference/authentication) for more information.

tags.name: preview
tags.description: Project dev previews (branch worktree dev servers)

tags.name: roles
tags.description: Roles and the actions they grant
